title: Dypsis humbertii
taxonomy: Dypsis humbertii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Arecaceae, commonly known as the palm family. It belongs to the genus Dypsis, which is a group of palms native to Madagascar. The genus Dypsis is further divided into several species, with Dypsis humbertii being one of the many species within the group. Dypsis humbertii is a unique species that is endemic to Madagascar, meaning it can be found nowhere else in the world. Through its classification, scientists can better understand the evolutionary relationships between different palm species.
